criteria.label: State the StandardScaler formula and what statistics are fit on training data.
criteria.example: Write z = (x − μ) / σ and note that μ and σ (ddof=1) are computed per column on X_train only.

criteria.label: Compute a MinMax-scaled value given train min and max.
criteria.example: Given train min = 0, max = 200, show x = 50 maps to (50 − 0)/(200 − 0) = 0.25 on [0, 1].

criteria.label: Implement train/test scaling without leakage in sklearn.
Name three algorithm families that need scaling and one that usually does not.
criteria.example: Need scaling: k-NN, Ridge, k-means. Usually skip: RandomForest on raw numeric features.

criteria.label: Contrast StandardScaler and MinMaxScaler for a heavy-tailed revenue column.
criteria.example: Explain that one $5M train outlier sets MinMax max and squeezes $20k–$80k rows near 0, while StandardScaler still leaves the outlier at high |z| and may inflate σ.
